sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After pressing UP, green-W moved from row 31 to row 28 (up by 3). So UP/DOWN also control green-W's vertical position. The movement is by 3 cells each step.
Now I understand the control scheme:
- LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN move the green-W frame by 3 cells
- green-W is the "player" avatar
Now the question is: what's the goal? Let me think about the layout:
- Left chamber with charcoal frame at (34, 13)
- Right chamber with charcoal frame at (28, 46)
- green-B at (31, 43) in the right chamber
- Purple wall separating the chambers
- Passage between chambers with a white 3x3 blocker at (31, 31)
The green-W frame is in the passage. It seems like the goal might be to:
1. Move green-W through the passage to one of the chambers
2. Possibly interact with the charcoal frames or green-B
Looking at the passage: there's a white 3x3 square at (31, 31) that blocks movement. But green-W can move vertically, so maybe I can go around it?
The passage seems to be between the two chambers. The purple wall is at col 36. The white blocker is at (31, 31). 
Let me think about the geometry:
- The passage connects the left and right chambers
- The white blocker is at (31, 31), which is in the middle of the passage
- green-W is currently at (28, 25) after moving UP
- The purple wall is at col 36
If I move RIGHT from (28, 25), green-W would go to col 28, then 31, then 34, then 37...
But the white blocker is at (31, 31), which is at a different row than green-W (currently at row 28). So maybe I can move past it!
Let me try moving RIGHT to get green-W through the passage to the right chamber.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# UP moved green-W from row 31 to row 28 (up by 3)
# So green-W moves by 3 in any direction
# Currently green-W is at row 28, col 25
# The white blocker is at row 31, col 31
# If I move RIGHT, green-W will be at row 28, avoiding the blocker at row 31!

# Let me try moving RIGHT to get past the blocker
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
